source_jobs.title: Head of Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
Fundwell is a financial technology platform revolutionizing the way businesses access and manage capital. Traditional business financing often involves complex processes, hidden fees, and lengthy approval times. We're changing that and fast. Leveraging our proprietary technology, deep industry expertise, and ethical-first approach, we deliver quick, transparent, and tailored funding solutions to small and medium-sized businesses nationwide. Today, our platform is expanding beyond financing into receivables, payables, and embedded payments, giving businesses greater control over their cash flow.

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Head of Partnerships to spearhead our strategic alliance and business development initiatives. This individual is responsible for identifying, negotiating, and managing key partnerships that are essential for driving company growth, strengthening our market position, and generating long-term value. The successful candidate will possess a robust background in business development, exceptional communication and negotiation abilities, and a demonstrable history of cultivating successful partnerships across diverse sectors.

How You’ll Make An Impact

 - Identify new partnership opportunities that will drive customer acquisition, market expansion, and revenue growth

 - Define, develop, and execute a comprehensive partnership roadmap & strategy

 - Drive incremental revenue through partner channels by developing go-to-market strategies, partner enablement programs, and co-marketing activities. Work closely with sales and marketing teams to ensure successful execution.

 - Track, analyze, and report on the performance of partnerships, including pipeline growth, partner-generated revenue, and other KPIs. Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership and stakeholders.

 - Represent the company at industry events, conferences, and partner meetings. Act as a thought leader and advocate for the company’s solutions in the partnership ecosystem

 - Manage and nurture existing partner relationships

What Sets You Apart

 - Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field (MBA preferred).

 - A minimum of 7 years of experience in partnerships, business development, or a similar role, specifically within a B2B SaaS environment.

 - Demonstrated success in establishing and expanding high-impact partnerships that significantly contribute to revenue growth and customer acquisition.

 - Extensive experience in negotiating complex agreements and strategically managing long-term partnerships.

 - Exceptional relationship-building capabilities, with the proven ability to collaborate effectively with both external partners and various internal stakeholders.

 - Comprehensive knowledge of SaaS sales cycles, go-to-market strategies, and the partner ecosystem.

 - Robust analytical skills for effectively tracking, reporting, and deriving insights from key performance indicators (KPIs) and return on investment (ROI) for partnership initiatives.

 - Outstanding communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.

 - The capacity to excel in a high-growth, fast-paced environment while expertly balancing and prioritizing multiple initiatives.

What Sets Fundwell Apart

 - Generous paid time off

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - Opportunities for professional growth and advancement within a rapidly growing fintech leader.

 - Collaborative, supportive team culture with a focus on training and development.

 - Prime office location in New York, NY.

Our Commitment to You

Fundwell is an equal employment opportunity employer. We adhere to a policy of making employment decisions without regard to age, race, color, creed, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation, sexual identification, ancestry, national origin, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil union partnership status, disability or handicap, veteran or military status (e.g. National Guard or Reserves).

source_jobs.title: AI Innovation Internship
source_jobs.description:
BUILD. COMPETE. WIN $25,000.


DURATION: JUNE – AUGUST 2026 (3 MONTHS)

Fundwell is a financial technology platform revolutionizing the way businesses access capital. Traditional business financing often involves complex processes, hidden fees, and lengthy approval times. We're changing that and fast. Leveraging our proprietary technology, deep industry expertise, and ethical-first approach, we deliver quick, transparent, and tailored funding solutions to small and medium-sized businesses nationwide. Today, our platform is expanding beyond financing into receivables, payables, and embedded payments, giving businesses greater control over their cash flow.




WHAT YOU'LL BE WORKING ON

We're accepting five interns, you'll be handed a real problem, a real budget, and the autonomy to solve it. We are hosting a 3 month long hackathon for interns to build something that can change the trajectory of Fundwell. Then we judge it, and someone will walk away with $25,000.

Your project will be scoped to your strengths and Fundwell's highest-leverage opportunities at the time. That means building AI-powered tools, automation workflows, and intelligent systems that create measurable impact across the business — from how we attract customers to how we build products.

Whatever your track, the expectation is the same: identify the opportunity, build the solution, demo your progress, and show results. We'll give you the resources. The rest is on you.




THE PROGRAM

From day one, you're building toward a final presentation to a panel of judges that includes our executive leadership. Throughout the summer, you'll run regular demos to show your progress and iterate based on feedback. At the end, the intern with the best results — measured by real impact, creativity, and execution.

Prizes:

 - 1st Place: $25,000 grand prize

 - 2nd Place: An all-expenses-paid trip for you and your friends**




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You're a student with a genuine obsession with AI and automation. You don't wait for permission to build things. You've probably already shipped something on your own, even if it's small.

You should apply if:

 - You're comfortable working with LLMs, APIs, MCP and automation tools

 - You thrive with open-ended problems and minimal hand-holding

 - You want real ownership, not busywork

 - You're genuinely competitive and the idea of a $25K prize makes you sharpen up

 - You have a background in CS, engineering, data science, or a related technical field is preferred, but we care more about what you've built than your major.


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

 - $20–25/hour base compensation

 - Latest-Gen Mac Mini workstation

 - Virtual spend card loaded with AI credits (Anthropic, OpenAI, and more)

 - A sandboxed environment to build freely — no red tape, no production risk

 - Direct access to Fundwell's leadership team

 - College credit coordination available upon request

 - A résumé line that actually means something

source_jobs.title: Head of Capital Markets
source_jobs.description:
Fundwell is a financial technology platform revolutionizing the way businesses access and manage capital. Traditional business financing often involves complex processes, hidden fees, and lengthy approval times. We're changing that and fast. Leveraging our proprietary technology, deep industry expertise, and ethical-first approach, we deliver quick, transparent, and tailored funding solutions to small and medium-sized businesses nationwide. Today, our platform is expanding beyond financing into receivables, payables, and embedded payments, giving businesses greater control over their cash flow.



THE ROLE

You will be the first capital markets hire and will own the entire funding stack - from financial modeling and lender origination through facility negotiation, closing, and ongoing compliance reporting. This is not a support role and it is not for someone who needs to be told what to do. You will sit at the table with warehouse lenders, family offices, and institutional investors, and you will be the person who builds the models they base their credit decisions on. You will work directly with the CEO on capital strategy and be a key voice in board-level discussions. We are looking for someone who is exceptionally sharp, moves fast, and has the intellectual horsepower to operate across financial modeling, legal negotiation, and investor relations simultaneously.

RESPONSIBILITIES

• Own the company financial model and FP&A function end-to-end: origination projections, portfolio roll-forward, payment waterfall, warehouse mechanics, P&L, cash flow, budgeting, and board reporting - maintaining it as the single source of truth for all capital raise and board discussions

• Build and maintain pro forma portfolio projections for each product channel (RBF, Term Loan, Card) including vintage-level performance tracking, collection curves, and chargeoff waterfalls

• Lead warehouse facility origination: identify target lenders (banks, credit funds, specialty platforms), build relationships, prepare pitch materials, and manage the full diligence process through commitment letter and closing

• Structure and negotiate facility terms: advance rates, interest rates, covenants, concentration limits, eligibility criteria, borrowing base mechanics, and early amortization triggers

• Source and close preferred equity subordination: engage family offices and institutional investors, structure terms, and manage ongoing investor communications

• Build and maintain lender reporting infrastructure: monthly borrowing base certificates, portfolio performance reports, covenant compliance dashboards, and quarterly investor updates

• Monitor facility utilization and capacity planning: forecast when facilities need upsizing, negotiate accordion features, and manage facility scaling as origination volume grows

• Partner with the CEO on capital strategy: equity raise timing, balance sheet optimization, securitization pathways, and credit facility diversification

• Lead ABS takeout planning: build the analytical framework for term securitization, evaluate timing and execution strategy, and position the portfolio for rated deal issuance

• Structure economics for channel partner and referral agreements, including commission structures, volume commitments, and holdback terms

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• 7+ years in capital markets, structured finance, or treasury at a specialty lending company, fintech lender, or credit fund - this is a senior role and we expect a senior track record

• Directly involved in closing at least one warehouse facility of $50M+ as borrower-side lead or a key member of the deal team - you have been through the full lifecycle from term sheet to closing docs

• Deep understanding of receivables-based lending economics: factor rates, MCA/RBF mechanics, payment waterfall structures, and SPV-level cash flow modeling

• Expert financial modeler: you have built warehouse facility models and pro forma portfolio projections that were rev

source_jobs.title: Sales Representative
source_jobs.description:
Fundwell is a financial technology platform revolutionizing the way businesses access capital. Traditional business financing often involves complex processes, hidden fees, and lengthy approval times. We're changing that and fast. Leveraging our proprietary technology, deep industry expertise, and ethical-first approach, we deliver quick, transparent, and tailored funding solutions to small and medium-sized businesses nationwide.

We're looking for a dynamic, results-driven Sales Representative to join our high-performing sales team in Tel Aviv. In this role, you'll engage directly with qualified leads, guiding them through our streamlined financing process and helping them secure the funding needed to drive their business forward.

How You'll Make an Impact

 - Conduct high volume cold calling outreach daily via phone and email and consistent follow-ups with prospects.

 - Demonstrate resilience and tenacity by handling rejection with confidence, learning from each interaction, and continuously refining your approach to convert challenging leads.

 - Manage and maintain your sales pipeline efficiently, moving leads from initial interest through funding.

 - Build and nurture strong relationships with business owners, becoming their trusted advisor and helping them navigate their funding options.

 - Collaborate closely with team members and sales leadership to consistently exceed sales targets.

 - Utilize Fundwell's proven playbook and technology to streamline your workflow and maximize your results.

 - Bring energy and determination to the team, contributing to a competitive, motivated, and supportive office culture where resilience and results are celebrated.

What Sets You Apart

 - Proven ability to engage prospects, overcome objections, and consistently close deals.

 - Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills; you thrive on building genuine relationships.

 - Highly self-motivated, energetic, and resilient under pressure.

 - Motivated by exceeding aggressive sales targets and achieving continuous growth.

 - A background in competitive environments such as athletics, hospitality, or similar fields is a plus.

 - Prior experience using CRM software (Salesforce or similar) to manage sales activities.

 - Ability to adapt quickly to new tools, processes, and sales strategies.

Why Fundwell?

 - Working Schedule Monday - Thursday, 4:00pm - 1:00am

 - Generous paid time off

 - Opportunities for professional growth and advancement within a rapidly growing fintech leader.

 - Collaborative, supportive team culture with a focus on training and development.

Our Commitment to You

Fundwell is an equal employment opportunity employer. We adhere to a policy of making employment decisions without regard to age, race, color, creed, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation, sexual identification, ancestry, national origin, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil union partnership status, disability or handicap, veteran or military status (e.g. National Guard or Reserves).

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Fundwell is a financial technology platform revolutionizing the way businesses access capital. Traditional business financing often involves complex processes, hidden fees, and lengthy approval times. We're changing that and fast. Leveraging our proprietary technology, deep industry expertise, and ethical-first approach, we deliver quick, transparent, and tailored funding solutions to small and medium-sized businesses nationwide. Today, our platform is expanding beyond financing into receivables, payables, and embedded payments, giving businesses greater control over their cash flow.

We're seeking a highly organized and proactive Executive Assistant to provide comprehensive support to our leadership team. This role requires exceptional discretion, flexibility, and the ability to manage both professional and personal tasks with equal care and attention to detail.

How You’ll Make An Impact

 - Manage complex calendars, scheduling meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements

 - Serve as primary point of contact between executives and internal/external stakeholders

 - Handle sensitive information with the highest level of confidentiality and discretion

 - Prioritize and manage multiple projects simultaneously with competing deadlines

 - Screen and respond to emails, calls, and correspondence on behalf of executives

 - Follows up with internal and external stakeholders to hold them accountable

 - Plan and execute company events, offsites, and team gatherings

 - Coordinate office operations and serve as liaison with building management and vendors

 - Support special projects and initiatives as assigned

What Sets You Apart

 - 5+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or similar role

 - Exceptional organizational skills with proven ability to prioritize and multitask

 - Outstanding written and verbal communication skills

 - High degree of professionalism, integrity, and discretion

 - Strong proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Sheets)

 - Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

 - Flexible schedule and willingness to handle occasional after-hours requests

Why Fundwell?

 - Generous paid time off

 - 401(k) retirement plan

 - Opportunities for professional growth and advancement within a rapidly growing fintech leader.

 - Collaborative, supportive team culture with a focus on training and development.

 - Prime office location in New York, NY. This person will be able to work remotely and come to the office from time to time when needed.

Our Commitment to You

Fundwell is an equal employment opportunity employer. We adhere to a policy of making employment decisions without regard to age, race, color, creed, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation, sexual identification, ancestry, national origin, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil union partnership status, disability or handicap, veteran or military status (e.g. National Guard or Reserves).
